description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with a symmetrical rectangular footprint and a pitched, tiled roof in a muted reddish-brown color. The facade features regularly spaced windows arranged in vertical columns, with each floor having a consistent window pattern. Balconies are present on both the front and rear sides, providing outdoor space for several units per floor. The building is constructed primarily from concrete or masonry, with a smooth, light beige exterior finish. The main entrance is centrally located and recessed, flanked by large windowed sections. The overall design is functional and utilitarian, typical of urban residential complexes built for efficiency and density.
description: A symmetrical, mid-rise apartment building with a tiled pitched roof, light beige facade, and regularly spaced windows and balconies on both front and rear sides.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance, multiple balconies, and a symmetrical arrangement of windows.
description_view_1: The left side view displays a flat facade with windows but no balconies or entrance features.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, with balconies and a similar window arrangement but no visible entrance.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing only windows and a plain wall.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in evenly spaced vertical columns, creating a highly symmetrical and repetitive pattern across all floors.
